_meta.description: QE Savings Lab — internal architecture knowledge base

services.name: Savings Account Service
services.description: Owns the lifecycle of savings accounts: creation, closure, status transitions, interest application.

services.name: Identity & Verification Service
services.description: Handles KYC (Know Your Customer) checks, document verification, and AML screening for onboarding.
responsibilities: Validate customer identity documents (passport, driving licence)
Screen against PEP and sanctions lists
Flag high-risk customers for manual review

services.name: Payment Processing Service
services.description: Processes deposits and withdrawals. Integrates with Faster Payments Scheme (FPS) and internal ledger.
responsibilities: Process inbound deposits (bank transfer, debit card)
Idempotency key management for duplicate prevention

services.name: Notification Service
services.description: Sends push notifications, SMS, and email to customers for account events.
responsibilities: Retry failed notifications with exponential backoff

services.name: Customer Profile Service
services.description: Single source of truth for customer profile data. Owned by CRM domain.
responsibilities: Store and serve customer personal details
Link financial products to customer record

services.name: Core Banking Ledger
services.description: Legacy core banking system. All balances are ultimately reconciled here. Synchronous calls are high-latency; prefer event-driven integration.
services.integration: IBM MQ (async events) + REST adapter (sync queries, 500ms P95 SLA)
knownLimitations: No real-time streaming — batch updates every 15 minutes
No sandbox — testing requires dedicated integration environment

services.name: API Gateway
services.description: Kong-based API Gateway. Handles auth (JWT), rate limiting, and routing.

downstreamSystems.name: HMRC ISA Reporting
downstreamSystems.description: Annual ISA subscription data reported to HMRC. File-based SFTP submission. Deadline: April 5 each tax year.
downstreamSystems.testingNotes: Sandbox available. Test file format must match HMRC schema v3.1. Use test NINOs.

downstreamSystems.name: Onfido
downstreamSystems.description: KYC / identity verification. Document upload + facial biometric checks.
downstreamSystems.testingNotes: Sandbox available. Predefined test documents produce pass/fail/refer outcomes.

downstreamSystems.name: Faster Payments Scheme (FPS)
downstreamSystems.description: Near real-time UK payment network. Used for withdrawals and inbound transfers.
downstreamSystems.testingNotes: FPS test environment available. Simulate settlement delays with test sort codes.

downstreamSystems.name: Credit Reference Agency (Equifax)
downstreamSystems.description: Soft credit check during onboarding for risk scoring. Not a hard search.
downstreamSystems.testingNotes: Test IDs available. Produces predefined risk scores (Low/Medium/High).

downstreamSystems.name: FCA RegData
downstreamSystems.description: Regulatory data submission portal. Product sales and complaints data submitted quarterly.
downstreamSystems.integrationPattern: Manual CSV upload + API (v2 in progress)
downstreamSystems.testingNotes: Submissions validated against FCA schema. No sandbox — use pre-submission validation tool.

knownRisks: Core Banking Ledger batch latency can cause balance discrepancies in real-time views
Onfido webhook delivery is not guaranteed — missed callbacks can leave accounts in pending state
ISA allowance check is performed at account open only — not rechecked on top-up
Daily withdrawal limit resets at midnight UTC — edge case around limit enforcement near reset
FPS settlement failures are not always retried — manual intervention may be required
Duplicate account opening requests within 30 seconds may bypass idempotency check (known bug SAS-441)

environments.local: Docker Compose — all services mocked except SAS and PPS
environments.dev: Full integration environment — Onfido sandbox, FPS test, mock CBL
environments.staging: Production-mirror — real CBL integration, limited FPS test volume
